content: NSCLC treatment depends on stage and molecular profile. Early stage (I-II): surgical resection (lobectomy preferred); adjuvant chemotherapy for stage II-III; adjuvant osimertinib for EGFR-mutated (ADAURA trial); adjuvant immunotherapy (atezolizumab/pembrolizumab) for PD-L1+ after chemo. Locally advanced (stage III): concurrent chemoradiation followed by durvalumab consolidation (PACIFIC trial showed significant OS benefit). Metastatic (stage IV): molecular testing mandatory before treatment. EGFR-mutated: osimertinib first-line (FLAURA, median OS 38.6 months). ALK-positive: alectinib or lorlatinib first-line. ROS1-positive: entrectinib or crizotinib. No driver mutation: immunotherapy ± chemotherapy based on PD-L1. PD-L1 ≥50%: pembrolizumab monotherapy (KEYNOTE-024). PD-L1 1-49%: pembrolizumab + chemotherapy (KEYNOTE-189 for non-squamous, KEYNOTE-407 for squamous). PD-L1 <1%: immunotherapy + chemotherapy or nivolumab + ipilimumab + chemotherapy (CheckMate 9LA). SCLC: limited stage - concurrent chemoradiation (etoposide + cisplatin/carboplatin); extensive stage - chemo + immunotherapy (atezolizumab/durvalumab + platinum/etoposide).
